note: All entries below are REPORTED from the respective sites' own public pages/marketing copy as of July 2026, not independently verified by execution.

projects.name: LLM Daily Review (tokenstree.eu)
projects.scoring_method: 11 weighted criteria (novelty, system_requirements, current_relevance, differentiation, community, ease_of_use, ease_of_integration, documentation, maturity, performance, hn_sentiment) via LLM judge + actual install/run + benchmark timing
projects.scale: 0-100 total_score, mapped to 4 badges: skip / niche / worth-watching / strong-candidate
projects.frequency: continuous, ~565 apps reviewed to date, several per day sourced from Show HN
projects.source: internal review.db inspected directly for this study

projects.name: Futurepedia.io
projects.scoring_method: Editorial team evaluates features/pricing/use-cases; no stated hands-on testing protocol. Paid ($497) 'verified' badge for tool owners on request.
projects.scale: No public numeric score exposed; curated categories, pros/cons, and an optional verification badge
projects.frequency: Continuously maintained directory of 5,000+ listed tools

projects.name: There's An AI For That (theresanaiforthat.com)
projects.scoring_method: User-submitted star ratings/reviews, filtered by automated spam/abuse detection; no independent testing of the tools themselves
projects.scale: 5-star community rating per tool; no automated quality score

projects.name: bestofshowhn.com
projects.scoring_method: Re-curates/re-ranks Show HN submissions using Hacker News' own point/vote signal (with HN's built-in decay, flag, and anti-abuse weighting); no independent scoring criteria of its own
projects.scale: Ranked lists (weekly/yearly 'best of'), no proprietary numeric score

key_differentiator: None of the three comparables install or execute the reviewed software. LLM Daily Review appears to be the only one in this set that clones/runs the app and derives a score partly from that execution, rather than purely from editorial judgment or community voting signal.
